audit_status.source_base: Greek Acts text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Stephen's Israel history speech, Abraham/Joseph/Moses/temple critique, resistance to Spirit, Son of Humanity vision, Stephen's death, Saul witness.

verses.restored: The high priest said, Are these things so?
He said, Men, siblings and fathers, hear. The Divine of glory appeared to our father Abraham while he was in Mesopotamia, before he lived in Haran,
and said to him, Go out from your land and your kindred, and come into the land that I will show you.
Then he went out from the land of the Chaldeans and lived in Haran. From there, after his father died, the Divine moved him into this land in which you now live.
He gave him no inheritance in it, not even a footstep, yet promised to give it to him for possession and to his seed after him, though he had no child.
The Divine spoke thus: his seed would be a sojourner in another land, and they would enslave and mistreat them four hundred years.
And the nation they serve I will judge, said the Divine, and after these things they will come out and serve me in this place.
He gave him the covenant of circumcision; and thus Abraham fathered Isaac and circumcised him on the eighth day, Isaac Jacob, and Jacob the twelve patriarchs.
The patriarchs, jealous of Joseph, sold him into Egypt; and the Divine was with him,
and rescued him from all his pressures, gave him grace and wisdom before Pharaoh king of Egypt, and appointed him ruler over Egypt and all his house.
A famine came upon all Egypt and Canaan, and great pressure, and our fathers were finding no food.
Hearing there was grain in Egypt, Jacob sent our fathers first.
On the second time Joseph was made known to his brothers, and Joseph's family became manifest to Pharaoh.
Joseph sent and called Jacob his father and all the kindred, seventy-five souls/lives.
Jacob went down into Egypt, and he died, he and our fathers.
They were carried over to Shechem and placed in the tomb Abraham had bought for a price of silver from the sons of Hamor in Shechem.
As the time of the promise drew near, which the Divine had confessed to Abraham, the people grew and multiplied in Egypt,
until another king arose over Egypt who did not know Joseph.
This one dealt craftily with our kin and mistreated the fathers, making them expose their infants so they would not be kept alive.
At that time Moses was born, and he was beautiful to the Divine; he was nourished three months in his father's house.
When he was exposed, Pharaoh's daughter took him up and raised him as her own son.
Moses was educated in all the wisdom of Egyptians, and he was powerful in his words and works.
When forty years of time were fulfilled for him, it came up upon his heart to visit his siblings, the children of Israel.
Seeing someone being wronged, he defended him and made justice for the oppressed one, striking the Egyptian.
He thought his siblings would understand that the Divine was giving them salvation through his hand, but they did not understand.
The next day he appeared to them as they were fighting and urged them toward peace, saying, Men, you are siblings; why do you wrong one another?
But the one wronging the neighbor pushed him away, saying, Who appointed you ruler and judge over us?
Do you want to kill me as you killed the Egyptian yesterday?
Moses fled at this word and became a sojourner in the land of Midian, where he fathered two sons.
When forty years were fulfilled, a messenger appeared to him in the wilderness of Mount Sinai, in a flame of fire in a bush.
Moses, seeing it, marveled at the vision; as he approached to observe, the voice of the Lord came:
I am the Divine of your fathers, the Divine of Abraham and Isaac and Jacob. Moses became trembling and did not dare to observe.
The Lord said to him, Loose the sandal from your feet, for the place on which you stand is holy ground.
Seeing, I saw the mistreatment of my people in Egypt, and I heard their groaning, and I came down to rescue them. Now come, I will send you into Egypt.
This Moses, whom they denied, saying, Who appointed you ruler and judge? this one the Divine sent as ruler and redeemer with the hand of the messenger who appeared to him in the bush.
This one led them out, doing wonders and signs in Egypt and in the Red Sea and in the wilderness forty years.
This is the Moses who said to the children of Israel, The Divine will raise up for you a prophet like me from your siblings.
This is the one who was in the assembly in the wilderness with the messenger speaking to him on Mount Sinai and with our fathers, who received living oracles to give to us.
Our fathers did not wish to become obedient to him, but pushed him away and turned in their hearts to Egypt,
saying to Aaron, Make us gods who will go before us; for this Moses who led us out from Egypt, we do not know what happened to him.
They made a calf in those days and brought sacrifice to the idol, and were rejoicing in the works of their hands.

The Divine turned and gave them over to serve the host of heaven, just as it is written in the scroll of the prophets: Did you bring me slain beasts and sacrifices forty years in the wilderness, house of Israel?
You took up the tent of Moloch and the star of your god Rephan, the figures you made to worship; and I will relocate you beyond Babylon.
Our fathers had the tent of witness in the wilderness, just as the One speaking to Moses commanded him to make it according to the pattern he had seen.
Receiving it in turn, our fathers brought it in with Joshua when they dispossessed the nations that the Divine drove out from the face of our fathers, until the days of David.
David found grace before the Divine and asked to find a dwelling for the house of Jacob.
But Solomon built him a house.
Yet the Most High does not dwell in handmade houses, just as the prophet says,
Heaven is my throne, and earth the footstool of my feet. What kind of house will you build for me, says the Lord, or what is the place of my rest?
Did not my hand make all these things?
Stiff-necked and uncircumcised in hearts and ears, you always resist the holy Spirit; as your fathers, so also you.
Which of the prophets did your fathers not pursue? They killed those who announced beforehand the coming of the Righteous One, of whom you have now become betrayers and murderers,
you who received the Law by arrangements of messengers and did not guard it.
Hearing these things, they were cut through in their hearts and were grinding their teeth at him.
But being full of holy Spirit, he looked intently into heaven and saw glory of the Divine and Jesus standing at the right hand of the Divine.
He said, Look, I behold the heavens opened and the Son of Humanity standing at the right hand of the Divine.
Crying out with a great voice, they held their ears and rushed with one accord upon him.
Throwing him outside the city, they stoned him. The witnesses laid aside their garments at the feet of a young man called Saul.
They were stoning Stephen as he called out and said, Lord Jesus, receive my spirit.
Falling to his knees, he cried with a great voice, Lord, do not set this sin/misalignment against them. Saying this, he fell asleep.
